
Graphics Engine Bible 2026
Volume II Supplement

NFS Most Wanted (2005), DX9 Injector Engineering,
and Legacy Renderer Modernization Roadmap

=====================================================================
Introduction
=====================================================================

This supplement defines the advanced DX9-focused direction for future
Graphics Engine Bible chapters.

Primary Focus Areas:

- DirectX 9 renderer architecture
- Injector engineering
- Shader replacement systems
- ENB-style rendering frameworks
- Deferred rendering in SM3.0
- NFS Most Wanted (2005) analysis
- Drift City modernization
- GTA San Andreas modernization
- Prism3D modernization

=====================================================================
Chapter Roadmap
=====================================================================

Part I
DX9 Graphics Engineering

Part II
DX9 Injector Architecture

Part III
Shader Replacement Systems

Part IV
Legacy Engine Modernization

Part V
Need for Speed Most Wanted Renderer Analysis

=====================================================================
1. Full IDirect3DDevice9 Hook Architecture
=====================================================================

Topics:

- VTable discovery
- Device acquisition
- Present hooks
- EndScene hooks
- Reset hooks
- Lost device handling
- Resource recreation

Example Hook Targets

IDirect3DDevice9::Present
IDirect3DDevice9::EndScene
IDirect3DDevice9::Reset
IDirect3DDevice9::SetPixelShader
IDirect3DDevice9::SetVertexShader

Engineering Coverage

- State interception
- Draw-call inspection
- Shader tracking
- Render target capture
- Depth buffer acquisition

=====================================================================
2. State Block Capture and Restoration
=====================================================================

One of the most important injector systems.

Goals

- Preserve game state
- Prevent rendering corruption
- Support compatibility

Workflow

Capture State
↓
Execute Effects
↓
Restore State

Topics

- IDirect3DStateBlock9
- Texture state preservation
- Sampler state restoration
- Shader restoration
- Render target restoration

=====================================================================
3. Shader Replacement Frameworks
=====================================================================

Core Components

Shader Discovery
↓
Hash Generation
↓
Database Lookup
↓
Replacement Selection
↓
Runtime Compilation

Coverage

- Vertex shaders
- Pixel shaders
- Material identification
- Runtime shader swapping

Applications

- HD lighting
- Better materials
- PBR conversion
- Water upgrades
- Vehicle shaders

=====================================================================
4. ENB/ReShade-Style Effect Chains
=====================================================================

Effect Stack

HDR
↓
Bloom
↓
SSAO
↓
SSR
↓
Volumetric Fog
↓
Color Grading
↓
Sharpening
↓
Present

Future Chapters Include

- Resource management
- Effect scheduling
- Buffer sharing
- Performance optimization

=====================================================================
5. Deferred Lighting in DirectX 9 SM3.0
=====================================================================

Topics

- MRT limitations
- G-buffer packing
- Light accumulation
- Stencil volumes
- HDR integration

Recommended Pipeline

Depth
↓
GBuffer
↓
Light Accumulation
↓
HDR
↓
Post Processing

Coverage

- Memory budgets
- Render target formats
- Bandwidth optimization

=====================================================================
6. Prism3D Modernization
=====================================================================

Target Games

ETS2
ATS
Legacy Prism3D titles

Topics

- Road materials
- Weather systems
- Reflection systems
- Lighting upgrades
- HDR conversion

Potential Upgrades

- GTAO
- SSR
- Volumetric fog
- Improved shadows

=====================================================================
7. Drift City Renderer Analysis
=====================================================================

Topics

- Scene rendering order
- Vehicle rendering
- Material systems
- Environment shaders
- UI integration

Modernization Topics

- HDR pipeline
- Better reflections
- AO integration
- Modern weather rendering
- Improved vehicle paint

=====================================================================
8. GTA San Andreas Modernization
=====================================================================

Topics

- RenderWare architecture
- World rendering
- Vehicle rendering
- Shadow systems
- Streaming architecture

Modernization Topics

- Deferred lighting
- HDR rendering
- Volumetric lighting
- SSR
- PBR-style materials

=====================================================================
9. Need for Speed Most Wanted (2005)
=====================================================================

Major Focus Area

Renderer Characteristics

- DirectX 9
- Heavy post processing
- Motion blur systems
- Reflection rendering
- Streaming world architecture

Future Analysis Topics

World Renderer
Vehicle Renderer
Shadow Renderer
Reflection Systems
Speed Effects
Motion Blur
Depth of Field
Road Shading
Weather Effects

=====================================================================
10. NFS MW Modernization Roadmap
=====================================================================

Phase 1

HDR Conversion

Phase 2

Bloom Modernization

Phase 3

Improved Reflections

Phase 4

Ambient Occlusion

Phase 5

SSR

Phase 6

Volumetrics

Phase 7

PBR Conversion

Phase 8

Temporal Effects

=====================================================================
11. HLSL Library Development
=====================================================================

Future Chapters Will Include

Lighting Functions
Shadow Functions
Normal Encoding
SSR
SSAO
Bloom
Tone Mapping
Color Grading
Fog
Volumetrics

Goal

Create a reusable DX9 SM3.0 shader library suitable for:

- NFS Most Wanted
- Drift City
- GTA SA
- Prism3D
- Custom DX9 engines

=====================================================================
Summary
=====================================================================

Future encyclopedia chapters will place major emphasis on DirectX 9
engineering and modernization, with NFS Most Wanted (2005) serving as
one of the primary case studies for renderer analysis, shader
replacement, post-processing injection, and modern graphics upgrades.

End of Supplement.
